events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10am   to  10:30am .

events.title: Family Time & Stay and Play
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under with their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:00am to 10:30am.  Activity stations will be set up for self-directed learning and play from 10:30am to 11:00am. Participants will be capped at the first 25 families.

events.title: Little Builders
events.description: A fun construction zone where toddlers and preschoolers can play, stack, and create with giant building blocks. Ages 1-5. Drop-in.

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program

Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: BenTastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! Register here or by calling 416-396-8939.

events.title: Arts & Crafts
events.description: Let your creativity loose at Cliffcrest Library with our Summer Wonder Arts & Crafts program! We'll be making different projects every week in July & August. All supplies will be provided and you get to take your creations home! Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: EarlyON Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 5 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment. This program is offered in partnership with the Malvern Family Resource Centre (MFRC) . This is a drop-in program. No registration required.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers.  Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00am. Babies, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play until 11:30am.

events.title: Wednesday Whimsy
events.description: For children ages 2-6 with their parents or caregivers. Join us for Wednesday Whimsy! Little ones ages 2-6 will enjoy a brand-new adventure each time. We'll spark imagination and learning through stories, crafts, songs, and playful activities. Get ready to wiggle, giggle, and discover something wonderful! No registration – but space is limited. Large groups and daycares cannot be accommodated.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.

events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: A Very Gru-Some Summer Movie Series
events.description: Experience the hit movies featuring supervillain turned secret agent, Gru and his group of colourful little minions this summer in our library's theatre! Movie will play at 2pm sharp! July 3 - Despicable Me. (2010) 95 min. Rated G  July 17 - Despicable Me 2. (2013) 98 min. Rated G July 31 - Despicable Me 3. (2017) 90 min. Rated G August 7 - Minions. (2015) 91 min. Rated G August 21 - Minions: The Rise of Gru. (2022) 88 min. Rated G Drop-in. No registration needed.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Bharatanatyam - Indian Classical Dance Performance
events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in. All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis.  Suitable for ages 6-12. Dash: May 3, June 7, July 5, August 9 LEGO: May 10, June 14, July 12, August 16 Snap Circuits: May 24, June 21, July 19, August 23 Sphero: May 31, June 28, July 26, August 30

events.title: SUNDAY FUN-DAY
events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.title: Sunday SNAP Circuits
events.description: Come join us and explore electricity and circuitry. Learn about circuits and gain hands on experience with SNAP Circuits! Feel the spark when you build your own circuit! For children ages 6-12. Drop in.

events.title: Curious Chaos Club
events.description: Step into The Curious Chaos Club- where creativity gets a little messy (in the best way!) Kids ages 6- 12 can build, create and let the chaos begin!

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore a different STEM-themed activity every Sunday the library is open. Topics include robotics, circuitry, building and more! For children aged 6-12 and their caregivers. Drop in.

events.title: Sunday Tech Time
events.description: Explore the world of circuitry and robots. Test out Snap Circuits, Dash robots, or Spheros. Ages 5 and up.

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Use our guides to build a creation or use your creativity to build what you want!

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits, LEGO, K'nex, Marble Run and Kids Craft Club. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday Fun-Day
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. For children ages 6-12. Drop-in. Space and supplies are limited.

events.title: Snap Circuits

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Do you love arts and crafts? Join us for an afternoon of crafting fun! For children ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​ Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book " Jane and the Blue Willow Princess " about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.&

events.title: Making Comics Camp with Kean Soo
events.description: Learn how to write and draw comics & graphic novels with Kean Soo, the award-winning creator of Jellaby and March Grand Prix!  Kean will lead you through the basics of creating fun, compelling characters, story outlines, and then he will show you how to combine everything together to lay out and draw your own comic!  Program runs for three days July 20th to July 22nd, from 1pm-3pm. events.title: Author Visit: Jane and the Blue Willow Princess with Catherine Little
events.description: “On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​” Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour

Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Sewing Basics
events.description: Learn to use the Bernette B05 Academy to make a small drawstring bag! Want to learn to sew or would like a refresher on how to use a sewing machine? Our intro to sewing course will cover the basics of threading the machine, basic stitches and more. We will be creating a small pouch. All materials will be provided. Children under 13 must be accompanied by an adult. Registration is required for this class and begins May 8th, 2026 at 12:30 pm. Note: Your spot will not be guaranteed after 5 minutes

events.title: Coding with Python IV
events.description: Python is an open source programming language that is used for creating web applications, web content, mathematics and system scripting. In this follow up course we will take our understanding of data structures to the next level as well as looking into the next stages of error handling (including making some custom error messages) and extending our knowledge of input/output to more than just the terminal. This class is for beginners but assumes you have a basic understanding of Python or have a

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. You'll also hear about landmark legal cases - both su

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. See how an image can be altered and repaired using patch tools such as the clone and heali

events.title: Let's Talk Time
events.description: Gain confidence in practicing your English-speaking skills Expand your networking list and connect with new friends Enhance your Knowledge about Canadian life and culture Let's Talk Time is a free hybrid (in-person and online) program. For registration and further information contact Faiza E. 416-268-3981 or fesho@rexdalewomen.org In partnership with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada, Rexdale Women's Centre & Toronto Public Library.

events.title: Candy Sculptures Art
events.description: Create with food and make your own artistic candy sculptures! Prepackaged candy will be provided.  Food allergy notice: Please be aware that food will be used in this program. Certain food ingredients may cause allergic reactions in some people.

events.title: Youth Hub: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.
